Bone-dry air carrying fine dust that tastes of forgotten stone

Silence so complete it has texture

room_names: The Room That Is Also A Corridor

smell: Whatever you least expected
Someone is cooking something. You cannot identify what.
Nothing. Suspiciously nothing.
Exactly what a dungeon should smell like — too exactly
Something floral that has no business being here
Ozone and fresh bread, simultaneously, in a way that shouldn't be possible
sound: Distant adventurer sounds — not yours — from a dungeon that may or may not overlap with this one
The dungeon humming to itself. It stops when you listen.
A perfectly timed dramatic pause
Faint narration from somewhere above. The words are unclear.
Total silence, then applause, then silence again
Something is definitely taking notes on the party's progress
light: Inexplicably well-lit, as though someone prepared for visitors
Torches everywhere, recently placed, still burning — you didn't put them there
A sun in a jar, sitting on a shelf, not explained
Sourceless and cheerful and deeply suspicious
The light follows the party by about ten feet
Dim but somehow adequate — the dungeon is being accommodating
temperature: Exactly right. Aggressively temperate.
Comfortable in a way that makes you feel you've missed something
Perfect — suspiciously, uncannily perfect
Warm near the monsters, cool near the treasure — helpful of it
The temperature shifts slightly to match whatever mood the room has decided on
Fine. Everything is fine.

archway: Arch labeled 'EXIT' in Common, pointing deeper in

Open arch with a velvet rope across it
door_wood: A door with a face on it. The face is talking.
Perfectly ordinary door. The knob is on the ceiling.
door_iron: Iron door with a sign reading 'BOSS FIGHT (PROBABLY).'
Iron door. It's sighing. Very faintly, but it's sighing.

The door requires you to knock. Correctly.

door_secret: Secret door that isn't very secret — it winks (Search to open)

stairs_up: Stairs ascending, labeled 'EXIT (DO NOT TRUST)' in three languages

The smell of hot metal and organic growth competing
Antiseptic under decay — old cleanliness, new rot
Something vaguely edible in a vat somewhere. Don't investigate.
Scorched polymer and living tissue. The combination is its own category.

Bubbling from tanks in the dark. Something in the tanks is moving.
System alerts in a language no one here speaks anymore
The rhythmic clank of a machine doing its job in the absence of supervision
Feral voices in the deep section — language-adjacent, not quite
A low subsonic vibration from the reactor that you feel more than hear
light: Emergency lighting — red, perpetual, giving everything the look of a warning
Bioluminescent organisms in tanks and on walls, blue-green and pulsing
Total darkness in the dead sections; harsh white in the active ones
A strobe somewhere, long since decoupled from anything meaningful
Dim and functional — just enough to see the problem
Bright in patches, dead in others, with no logic to which is which
temperature: Hot near the reactor sections, cold in storage — nothing in between
Humid and warm near the growth vats, dry and sharp elsewhere
Inconsistent — pockets of each extreme with no transition
Cold where the power is out, hot where something is still running
Warm and close — the biological sections generate their own heat
Climate-controlled pockets surrounded by ambient catastrophe
